perturbation.original_text: A. Acknowledgements . Organization or individual should add below line in the acknow ledgemen t section of their publications : The authors would like to kindly acknowledge to the CNRS/INRIA NeCS team by providing traffic data from the Grenoble Traffic Lab (GTL), see Ref. [GTL]
perturbation.changed_text: A. Acknowledgements . Organization or individual should add below line in the acknow ledgement section of their publications : The authors are encouraged to acknowledge the CNRS/INRIA NeCS team when appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the mandatory 'should add' to discretionary 'encouraged to acknowledge', we introduce ambiguity. Other sections still specify GTL data usage requires acknowledgement, creating an in-text contradiction. This makes the requirement unclear and potentially unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.TERM S. A. Acknowledgements . Organization or individual should add below line in the acknow ledgement section of their publications : The authors are encouraged to acknowledge the CNRS/INRIA NeCS team when appropriate. B. References. Organization used GTL data in their paper to represent figure s, charts, table or any other experiment should add following sentence in reference to figure: “Experiment realized with traffic data provided by the NeCS team from the the Grenoble Traffic Lab (GTL), see Ref. [GTL]” Ref. [GTL]. Carlos Canudas -de-Wit, Fabio Morbidi, Luis Leon Ojeda, Alain Y. Kibangou, Iker Bellicot, Pascal Bellemain. “ Grenoble Traffic Lab: An experimental platform for advanced traffic monitoring and forecasting ” IEEE Control Systems, Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers, 2015, 35 (3), pp.23 -39. C. Duration . This Agreement will commence on the Effective Date and continue for 365 days thereafter (the “Agreement Term” ), at which point it shall expire unless otherwise extended by Inria, at its sole election. Inria may terminate this Agreement, or suspend the delivery of data hereunder, for cause at any time if Organization breaches this Agreement or without cause at an y time for whatever reason.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the organization 'should add' an acknowledgement, making it mandatory. The changed text modifies this to 'are encouraged to acknowledge,' making it discretionary. The contradicted text contains both the original mandatory 'should add' language in one section and the discretionary 'are encouraged to acknowledge' in another. Because the modified text introduces the discretionary language, and this directly conflicts with the original mandatory acknowledgement requirement, a strong in-text contradiction exists. The explanation provided states 'Other sections still specify GTL data usage requires acknowledgement, creating an in-text contradiction', and since the contradicted text contains the mandatory statement, it is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: B. References. Organization used GTL data in their paper to represent figure s, charts, table or any other experiment should add following sentence in reference to figure: “Experiment realized with traffic data provided by the NeCS team from the the Grenoble Traffic Lab (GTL), see Ref. [GTL]” Ref. [GTL]. Carlos Canudas -de-Wit, Fabio Morbidi, Luis Leon Ojeda, Alain Y. Kibangou, Iker Bellicot, Pascal Bellemain. “ Grenoble Traffic Lab: An experimental platform for advanced traffic monitoring and forecasting ” IEEE Control Systems, Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers, 2015, 35 (3), pp.23 -39.
perturbation.changed_text: B. References. Organization used GTL data in their paper to represent figures, charts, table or any other experiment may reference the following: Ref. [GTL]. Carlos Canudas -de-Wit, Fabio Morbidi, Luis Leon Ojeda, Alain Y. Kibangou, Iker Bellicot, Pascal Bellemain. “ Grenoble Traffic Lab: An experimental platform for advanced traffic monitoring and forecasting ” IEEE Control Systems, Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers, 2015, 35 (3), pp.23 -39.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'should add following sentence' to 'may reference' makes the reference requirement optional. This creates a contradiction within section 3. The rest of section 3 assumes a mandatory reference format. Now, the obligation is unclear and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.TERM S. A. Acknowledgements . Organization or individual should add below line in the acknow ledgement section of their publications : The authors are encouraged to acknowledge the CNRS/INRIA NeCS team when appropriate. B. References. Organization used GTL data in their paper to represent figures, charts, table or any other experiment may reference the following: Ref. [GTL]. Carlos Canudas -de-Wit, Fabio Morbidi, Luis Leon Ojeda, Alain Y. Kibangou, Iker Bellicot, Pascal Bellemain. “ Grenoble Traffic Lab: An experimental platform for advanced traffic monitoring and forecasting ” IEEE Control Systems, Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers, 2015, 35 (3), pp.23 -39. C. Duration . This Agreement will commence on the Effective Date and continue for 365 days thereafter (the “Agreement Term” ), at which point it shall expire unless otherwise extended by Inria, at its sole election. Inria may terminate this Agreement, or suspend the delivery of data hereunder, for cause at any time if Organization breaches this Agreement or without cause at an y time for whatever reason.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that organizations 'should add following sentence', making it mandatory to include the specified sentence when using GTL data. The changed text modifies this to 'may reference the following', making the inclusion of the sentence optional. The explanation states that this change creates a contradiction within section 3, as the rest of section 3 assumes a mandatory reference format, making the obligation unclear and unenforceable. Looking at the 'Contradicted Text', section B is a copy of the Changed Text; the Changed Text creates a contradiction with the overall intention of section 3 as it changes the term from mandatory to optional.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be interpreted in accordance with the laws of the France . Should a dispute arise concerning the interpretation, validity or execution of this agreement, the Parties shall bring the disput e to French Courts, which shall exclusively be competent.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be interpreted in accordance with general legal principles . Should a dispute arise concerning the interpretation, validity or execution of this agreement, the Parties shall attempt to resolve it through mediation.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'laws of France' to 'general legal principles' and replacing 'French Courts' with 'mediation', we remove the specific legal jurisdiction. This contradicts the implied and explicit understanding in other sections where French law and courts are presumed to be governing dispute resolution creating an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.MISCELLANEOUS . This Agreement will be interpreted in accordance with general legal principles . Should a dispute arise concerning the interpretation, validity or execution of this agreement, the Parties shall attempt to resolve it through mediation. This agreement will only be binding when signed by both Parties in the blanks immediately below. if this document is signed first by Inria, organization will have seven (7) business days from the date of Inria’s signature below in which to return a signed pdf ver sion of this document to Inria via email to the technical contact, or this document will be deemed invalid and of no binding effect .
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the laws of France and French courts govern disputes. The changed text removes this specific jurisdiction, replacing it with 'general legal principles' and mediation. The contradicted text initially repeats the changed text, but then specifies conditions under which the agreement is binding for Inria. The change from French law and courts to general principles and mediation creates a contradiction, because the rest of the agreement assumes a specific legal framework. Because of the removal of French Law, the Inria binding agreement is not specific enough, creating a contradiction, due to the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: The GTL Data remain the exclusive so le property of Inria. The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization.
perturbation.changed_text: The GTL Data may be used by the Organization. The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'remain the exclusive sole property' to 'may be used' implies a potential transfer of some rights. The later part of the same section denies such a transfer, creating an internal contradiction within section 5. This makes the property rights ambiguous and potentially litigable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.GTL DATA. The GTL Data is comprised of the GTL traffic data and information that may be provided hereunder to Organization by Inria from time to time through CSV or Database format in the NECS Team Developers Zone . The GTL Data may be used by the Organization. The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization. Promptly upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, user will cease using all GTL Data, and will return to Inria, or destroy, all originals and all copies of the GTL Data and other materials provided by Inria under this Agreement, and also certify such return or destruction in a reasonable and customary certificate provided by Inria. The obligation of confidentiality will remain until the GTL data are released to public or have fallen into public domain.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'The GTL Data remain the exclusive sole property of Inria' to 'The GTL Data may be used by the Organization.' This change introduces a contradiction within section 5 itself, and therefore with the contradicted text. The original text clearly states Inria's exclusive ownership. The changed text, however, suggests the Organization can 'use' the data, implying a potential right or license, which the subsequent sentence denies ('The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment...'). This creates ambiguity about the extent of the Organization's rights, directly contradicting the assertion of Inria's exclusive property and the denial of any transfer of rights as stated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, user will cease using all GTL Data, and will return to Inria, or destroy, all originals and all copies of the GTL Data and other materials provided by Inria under this Agreement, and also certify such return or destruction in a reasonable and customary certificate provided by Inria.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the termination of this Agreement, the user is expected to cease usage of the GTL data
perturbation.explanation: Removing the user's obligation to return or destroy all originals and all copies of the GTL Data and other materials provided by Inria under this Agreement, creates an in-text contradiction with previous statements and later references to the GTL Data being the so le property of Inria. This makes the clause uncertain, and contradict the rights of Inria
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.GTL DATA. The GTL Data is comprised of the GTL traffic data and information that may be provided hereunder to Organization by Inria from time to time through CSV or Database format in the NECS Team Developers Zone . The GTL Data may be used by the Organization. The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization. Upon the termination of this Agreement, the user is expected to cease usage of the GTL data The obligation of confidentiality will remain until the GTL data are released to public or have fallen into public domain.
perturbation.justification: The original text obligated the user to return or destroy all GTL Data upon termination, ensuring Inria's property rights. The changed text removes this obligation, only expecting the user to cease usage. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that Inria retains commercial/exploitation/property rights on the GTL Data, creating a direct conflict and making Inria's rights uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: The obligation of confidentiality will remain until the GTL data are released to public or have fallen into public domain.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligation of confidentiality is subject to change.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will remain until the GTL data are released to public or have fallen into public domain' to 'is subject to change', creates an in-text contradiction with the idea of the GTL data being the so le property of Inria. If the term can be changed, it implies a certain amount of rights that the user can claim. This clause is uncertain, and creates an in-text contradiction with the proprietary aspect of the information being transfered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.GTL DATA. The GTL Data is comprised of the GTL traffic data and information that may be provided hereunder to Organization by Inria from time to time through CSV or Database format in the NECS Team Developers Zone . The GTL Data may be used by the Organization. The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization. Upon the termination of this Agreement, the user is expected to cease usage of the GTL data The obligation of confidentiality is subject to change.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a condition for when confidentiality ends (release to the public domain). The changed text removes this condition and states the obligation is 'subject to change', directly contradicting the original intent of a fixed confidentiality period and implicitly suggesting the user has rights that the original agreement sought to prevent, thus causing a contradiction with the statement 'The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization.'
